meta.title: FSL Use-Case Taxonomy
meta.notes: Canonical source for the use-case corpus; supersedes the category list inside 2026-06-11-fsl-use-case-taxonomy.md (which retains the SEO mechanics prose). v2 expanded v1's 202 categories; v3 adds six supergroups (XIX-XXIV: public safety & emergency, manufacturing/maintenance/reliability, sports & competition, telecom & carrier, open source & standards governance, personal productivity & life). Each category becomes a web hub page; each seed grows into a use-case-template instance (see 2026-06-11-fsl-manual-templates.md). anchor=true marks the user's original anchor terms. Seeds set scale, not bounds. Never delete a category with instances - redirect. Deliberate omissions by taste, not oversight: weapons/defense targeting, adult industry.

schema.supergroups: [{id, ordinal, title, categories: [{id, title, anchor?, seeds: [string]}]}]
schema.collections: [{id, title, purpose, items: [{id, title, kills, size: tiny|small|medium}]}] - curated cross-cutting lists; items map to use-case-template instances; tiny items' editor projection is essentially the whole page

collections.title: Everyday Frontend Wins
collections.purpose: Small and medium machines every frontend engineer has written badly at least twice. Each item is named by the bug it kills. Conversion material: recognition, not persuasion.

collections.title: Everyday Gamedev Wins
collections.purpose: Game developers already live in state machines (Animator controllers, behavior trees, audio states) - the pitch is not 'use an FSM' but 'yours, but provable, with deterministic replay.' Every item names a bug with folklore status. The replay/desync items are the native FSL angle: the tape IS the replay.

collections.title: The Interview Canon, But Provable
collections.purpose: Every system-design classic, except the whiteboard answer comes with a checker. The collection where students and interviewers meet FSL.

items.title: The elevator
items.kills: the whiteboard answer that starves floor 3 forever

items.title: The parking lot
items.kills: the design with no answer for the car that never leaves

items.title: The vending machine

items.title: The traffic light

items.title: The turnstile

items.title: The ATM
items.kills: dispensing before debiting; the card eaten in a half-finished session

items.title: The TCP handshake
items.kills: the interview hand-wave over SYN-RECEIVED

items.title: Tic-tac-toe

items.title: The washing machine

collections.title: CS Topics, Taught by Machines
collections.purpose: Each machine EMBODIES a concept rather than describing it; here the kills field names the misconception it kills. Pairs with learn-by-repairing (break the machine, hand the student the counterexample) and the §17 zoo. The checker is the teaching assistant: wrong intuitions become reachable states you can visit.

items.title: NFA-to-DFA subset construction, live
items.kills: 'nondeterminism adds power' - watch determinization preserve the language while exploding the states

items.title: The pumping lemma machine
items.kills: 'regex can match balanced parentheses' - watch the loop pump you out of the language

items.title: The bracket matcher on the pushdown rung
items.kills: 'it's all just pattern matching' - the exact line where regular dies and the stack begins

items.title: Bisimulation & minimization
items.kills: 'different diagrams mean different behavior' - 40 states collapse to 12 before your eyes

items.title: Product construction
items.kills: 'checking two properties needs two passes' - the intersection automaton is one machine

items.title: Ten toggles (the explosion demo)
items.kills: 'we'll just enumerate the states' - ten booleans, 1,024 configurations, one humbled student

items.title: The decidability tripwires
items.kills: 'computers can analyze anything' - add a second stack or a zero-test and watch the checker refuse, for a reason it can name

items.title: Two stacks = a Turing machine
items.kills: 'Turing machines are exotic' - they are two pushdowns in a trenchcoat

items.title: Totality vs the halting problem
items.kills: 'just check if it loops' - why FSL forbids recursion so that every question stays answerable

items.title: KMP's failure function IS an automaton
items.kills: 'substring search needs nested loops' - the pattern compiles to a machine that never backs up

items.title: Huffman decoding as a bit-walk
items.kills: 'compression is magic' - decoding is an automaton walking a tree, one bit per edge

items.title: Backtracking vs DFA regex
items.kills: 'regex is always fast' - the same pattern, catastrophic on one engine, linear on the machine

items.title: Recursive descent = a pushdown in disguise
items.kills: 'parsing is splitting on spaces' - the call stack was the automaton's stack all along

items.title: Promise states
items.kills: 'you can resolve twice' - pending/fulfilled/rejected with the one-way doors made visible

items.title: The event loop as a machine
items.kills: 'await pauses the computer' - tasks, microtasks, and why your timeout fired late

items.title: Peterson's algorithm
items.kills: 'a boolean flag is a lock' - the checker finds the interleaving your intuition missed

items.title: Dining philosophers
items.kills: 'it works on my machine' - deadlock as a reachable state with a replayable path to it

items.title: The TCP state diagram, honestly
items.kills: 'connections just open and close' - TIME_WAIT exists and now you know why

items.title: Two generals
items.kills: 'just add one more ack' - the checker proves NO protocol works; teaching a negative result as a fact, not a vibe

items.title: Raft leader election
items.kills: 'pick the server with the highest ID' - terms, votes, and split elections you can replay

items.title: Vector clocks
items.kills: 'timestamps order events' - concurrent means concurrent, and the clock proves it

items.title: Exponential backoff with jitter
items.kills: 'retry immediately' - watch synchronized retries DDoS the simulation, then add jitter

items.title: MESI cache coherence
items.kills: 'memory is just an array' - four states per cache line and the bus traffic between them

items.title: Tri-color garbage collection
items.kills: 'GC just frees unused stuff' - the tri-color invariant as an actual invariant the checker holds

items.title: Page states & faults
items.kills: 'RAM is RAM' - resident, swapped, dirty, and the fault that brings a page home

items.title: Process scheduler states
items.kills: 'the OS runs programs in order' - ready/running/blocked and the preemption that interleaves everything

items.title: CPU pipeline hazards
items.kills: 'one instruction at a time' - stalls, forwarding, and the bubble you can watch propagate

items.title: Compiler phases as a pipeline of machines
items.kills: 'the compiler reads my code once, top to bottom' - lex, parse, check, lower: four machines holding hands

items.title: The gambler's-fallacy machine
items.kills: 'randomness has memory' - a Markov chain whose statistics converge while every run stays wild

items.title: Monte Carlo estimation
items.kills: 'we need the exact answer' - seeded walks with confidence intervals; precision as a budget

items.title: Hamming-code correction as a machine
items.kills: 'corrupted data is lost data' - the syndrome walks straight to the flipped bit

items.title: Diffie-Hellman, step by step
items.kills: 'you can't agree on a secret in public' - the exchange as states, with the eavesdropper's view as a second machine that provably learns nothing useful
